concepts.title: Time Complexity of Push and Pop Operations
concepts.description: The time complexity of push and pop operations in a stack is O(1).

concepts.title: Stack Operations
concepts.description: A stack supports the following operations: push, pop, peek, isEmpty, isFull.

concepts.title: Traversal of Stack
concepts.description: Stacks do not support traversal. It is a Last In First Out (LIFO) data structure.

concepts.title: Stack Operations Mnemonic
concepts.description: To remember the order of stack operations: 'PUSH' - Push operation, 'POP' - Pop operation, 'PEEK' - Peek operation.

concepts.title: Stack Overflow Condition
concepts.description: Students often forget to check if the stack is full before pushing an element. Always check the stack's capacity before pushing an element.

concepts.title: Stack Implementation
concepts.description: A stack can be implemented using an array or linked list. The choice of implementation depends on the specific requirements of the application.

concepts.title: Advantages of Stack
concepts.description: Stacks have several advantages, including efficient use of memory, simple implementation, and fast execution of operations.

concepts.title: Real-World Applications of Stack
concepts.description: Stacks have many real-world applications, including parsing expressions, evaluating postfix notation, and implementing recursive algorithms iteratively.
